question_text: A gas has a pressure of 100 kPa when its volume is 50 cm³. Using Boyle's law, calculate the pressure when the volume is reduced to 25 cm³, assuming the temperature remains constant.
explanation: Using Boyle's law: P₁V₁ = P₂V₂, so 100 × 50 = P₂ × 25. This gives P₂ = 5000 / 25 = 200 kPa. The volume has been halved, so the pressure doubles.
